sections.title: Revenue declined 11.9% month-over-month in February 2024
sections.narrative: Revenue fell from $1,103,810 in January to $972,410 in February, a decrease of $131,399 (-11.9%). This reversal followed strong growth in January and preceded recovery in March.

sections.title: South region drove the decline with $153K revenue loss
sections.narrative: While North, West, and East regions showed minimal changes or slight increases, the South region experienced a significant revenue drop of $153,134, accounting for 116% of the total decline (other regions offset some of the loss).

sections.title: Online channel in South region contributed 50% of the decline
sections.narrative: Within the South region, the Online channel saw the largest drop at $65,732, followed by Retail Store ($47,996) and Mobile App ($39,406).

visuals.title: Revenue Change by Channel in South Region (Jan-Feb 2024)

sections.title: Enterprise customers in South Online drove 37% of the channel decline
sections.narrative: Among customer segments in South Online, Enterprise showed the largest decrease at $24,122, followed by Consumer ($21,219) and Business ($20,391).

visuals.title: Revenue Change by Customer Segment in South Online (Jan-Feb 2024)

sections.title: Electronics category was the primary product driver of the decline
sections.narrative: Within South Online Enterprise, Electronics products declined by $13,574, representing 56% of the segment's decline. Home & Kitchen followed with a $6,689 drop.

visuals.title: Revenue Change by Product Category in South Online Enterprise (Jan-Feb 2024)
